30: details of the provisions of the us envoy s proposal witkoff and the mediators and the responses of hamas and the occupation the mediators proposal was delivered to hamas and israel yesterday in doha after an intensive round of negotiations the mediators proposal included four provisions that formed a framework for agreeing to a permanent ceasefire the mediators proposal included hamas releasing five living israeli prisoners on the first day including idan alexander in exchange for hamas releasing the five prisoners an agreement was reached to release a number of palestinian prisoners the mediators proposal stipulated that the handover of the five prisoners would be followed by indirect negotiations within 50 days the indirect negotiations following the handover of the five prisoners would discuss a permanent ceasefire and prisoner exchange

what is the fate of the ceasefire negotiations in gaza the qatari foreign ministry stated that qatar and egypt are continuing their intensive mediation efforts to reach an agreement that will end the war in gaza ensure the protection of civilians and exchange detainees and prisoners he noted some progress made in the latest round of intensive negotiations on gaza which lasted three weeks he explained that suspending negotiations for consultations before resuming dialogue is normal in the context of complex negotiations in a related context qatar and egypt called for not being swayed by media leaks that undermine efforts and influence the negotiations it also called on the international media not to undermine efforts to end the war but rather to highlight the unprecedented suffering in gaza egypt and qatar in partnership with the united states affirmed their commitment to continuing efforts to reach a comprehensive agreement in gaza

al arabi tv the first phase of the agreement proposal includes the return of the displaced to their areas of residence and the withdrawal of israeli forces from the gaza valley the agreement proposal stipulates in its first phase the entry of sufficient quantities of aid fuel and relief materials and the release of 50 palestinian prisoners for every female soldier detained by hamas the agreement proposal includes in the first stage the release of the prisoners of the shalit deal and the lifting of all punitive measures against the prisoners the second phase witnesses hamas releasing male israeli detainees in exchange for an agreed upon number of palestinian prisoners the third phase of the agreement proposal includes starting the implementation of a reconstruction plan for a period of 3 to 5 years and completely ending the siege of the gaza strip the guarantors of the ceasefire agreement proposal are qatar egypt the united states and the united nations the agreement proposal stipulates the immediate start of the rehabilitation of infrastructure hospitals and bakeries

breaking lawyer khaled mahajna the palestinian prisoners in israel s sde teiman detention camp are detained in 4 encampments in each encampment there are 100 to 150 detainees sleeping on the floor without blankets and without any pillows more than a thousand palestinians from gaza are detained in sde teiman detention camp living in tragic conditions palestinian detainees in sde teiman are handcuffed and blindfolded all day the occupation prevents prisoners from practicing religious rituals

prisoners authority and prisoners club underground visit first for gaza detainees the first legal visits were conducted for gaza detainees held in the rakefet section of nitzan prison in ramle the section is considered one of the harshest detention facilities where detainees are subjected to strict supervision and inhumane conditions many detainees described being subjected to systematic torture such as interrogation using disco and pampers methods starvation and humiliation detainees suffer from denial of healthcare physical and psychological abuse and complete isolation from the outside world the rakefet section includes surveillance cameras inside the cells prohibits prayer and imposes humiliating and degrading treatment common torture methods include broken fingers physical assault medical neglect and starvation detainees are forced to curse their mothers and are forbidden from telling the time or seeing the sun cleanliness and clothing conditions are extremely poor with restricted access to showers and bathrooms the occupation uses several camps and prisons to torture gaza detainees most notably sde teiman anatot ofer rakefet and menashe as of april 2025 the number of gaza detainees recognized by the israeli prison service reached 1 747 this number does not include those in military camps

gaza municipality 75 of water wells and more than 100 000 linear meters of water networks were damaged due to the israeli war we are facing extreme difficulty in delivering water to the displaced due to the occupation s destruction of water and sewage networks

the extent of the damage to the infrastructure and property of the nuseirat municipality as stated by its mayor dr iyad maghari about 25 000 square meters of main and secondary roads and streets as well as 5 main squares and intersections were destroyed destruction of 12 water wells about 4 000 linear meters of water networks 15 water valves and 2 desalination plants 10 electricity generators that operate generators to pump water into the network and 6 solar energy systems that serve municipal facilities were destroyed about 20 000 tons of waste have accumulated in the streets and around shelter centers due to the depletion of fuel the destruction of a number of municipal vehicles and the prevention of municipal crews from reaching a number of areas in nuseirat destruction of 3 550 linear meters of sewage networks 650 linear meters of rainwater drainage networks and 2 sewage stations five vehicles of different sizes were bombed most of which collect and transport waste and follow up on and address sanitation complaints about 6 600 trees were also destroyed from public streets the municipal nursery and the main municipal park the municipality of nuseirat suffers from a significant scarcity of diesel needed to operate water wells and pump it into the network in addition to operating water stations sewage treatment sewage water is overflowing in some of the streets of nuseirat as a result of the scarcity of fuel and the occupation s destruction of the main water and sewage lines as part of its deliberate targeting of the infrastructure as well as the failure of the jetter mechanism necessary to sweep and clear the sewage lines

government media office 1 2 the israeli occupation s decision to ban unrwa activities in the occupied palestinian lands represents a historical catastrophe and a shock to millions of palestinian refugees and an outright challenge to the united nations and international law we demand the expulsion of the israeli occupation from international and un institutions the israeli occupation continues to commit numerous legal violations and acts of aggression against international resolutions which are considered crimes in violation of international law the latest of these actions is the occupation s formal decision to ban the activities of the united nations relief and works agency for palestine refugees unrwa in the occupied palestinian lands a move condemned by various countries and international organizations the occupation has officially notified the united nations of its decision to cut ties with unrwa marking a historical catastrophe and a profound shock to millions of palestinian refugees within and outside palestine who have awaited their return to their occupied lands since 1948 in accordance with international resolutions this decision also constitutes a direct challenge to the united nations and international law undermining international legitimacy this dangerous catastrophic decision by the occupation specifically targets the services unrwa provides to millions of palestinian refugees including targeting educational services unrwa operates an extensive network of schools offering primary intermediate and secondary education to palestinian refugees alongside vocational and technical training programs unrwa schools are among the most essential educational resources providing free education to hundreds of thousands of palestinian students targeting healthcare services the occupation s decision targets health services provided by unrwa which operates health centers delivering primary care preventive and therapeutic health services to millions of palestinian refugees these services include vaccinations maternal and child health mental health care chronic disease management and more targeting relief and social services the ban impacts unrwa s social and relief services which support the neediest families with food aid financial assistance and support for those living below the poverty line targeting infrastructure and camp improvement the decision also impacts unrwa s infrastructure improvement projects in refugee camps such as upgrading water and sanitation networks developing roads and buildings and enhancing the overall living standards within these camps targeting employment and job programs unrwa provides employment opportunities for refugees through operational programs and vocational training programs enabling them to achieve self reliance targeting psychosocial support services the ban affects psychosocial support services for children and youth impacted by conflicts and displacement including recreational and educational programs aimed at alleviating psychological stress targeting emergency response services the decision will impact emergency response services in times of crisis and conflict affecting the distribution of food and non food items as well as urgent medical care

capping off their participation in the mashreq maghreb social forum held this week in addition to meeting with several groups popular front for the liberation of palestine deputy secretary general jamil mezher delivered a speech stating today palestine writes with its blood flesh pain suffering and heroism creating an unprecedented heroic epic in the national palestinian struggle he thanked the tunisian people for their embrace emphasizing their support for the palestinian resistance mezher expressed his pride in our martyrs and in the resistance that shattered the zionist enemy saluting the palestinian people and their resistance in the face of displacement killing destruction and genocide mezher stated the courageous resistance represented by all the military wings of the resistance forces is fighting this battle and will not stop until this aggression and the ongoing war of genocide against the palestinian people cease the resistance s position is firm and clear and it will not compromise or succumb to this zionist enemy our people will continue to fight and remain steadfast on their land foiling all the zionist enemy s plans of displacement genocide starvation and domination the enemy has failed to achieve any of its goals from the aggression on gaza which have been thwarted by our people s steadfastness and the bravery of the resistance mezher reiterated the resistance s adherence to its clear conditions ceasefire withdrawal lifting siege aid entry and reconstruction he stressed that the resistance will continue until these goals are achieved and the occupation is hindering the proposal that was accepted by the resistance factions mezher saluted the axis of resistance especially in lebanon yemen and iran as well as south africa he called for efforts to condemn and criminalize normalization stating that al aqsa flood dealt a strong blow to normalization and attempts of imperialism and its partners to integrate this enemy entity into the region he called for greater popular mobilization stating it is inconceivable that universities in america and europe rise up while there is a state of dormancy and lethargy in the arab region therefore we call on the arab masses for urgent action and to take to the squares to support our people the resistance and to denounce the genocide war waged against our people mezher renewed full confidence that a decisive victory will be the ally of our palestinian people and their resistance pledging to the martyrs our people and the arab masses that the palestinian resistance will triumph in this battle and that the enemy is doomed to vanish hamas leader sami abu zuhri also thanked tunisia s leadership and people for their stance he stated the battle of al aqsa flood achieved its goals from its very first hours no matter what destruction and killing the occupation commits it will not succeed in washing away its disgrace and defeat this battle succeeded in setting the occupation on the path to its end and its repercussions have contributed to reshaping global opinion in favor of the palestinian people with the intifada in universities worldwide being clear evidence of this hamas and the resistance factions are immune to the pressures and military escalation of the occupation and cannot accept its conditions rather in the end the occupation will succumb to their conditions the resistance is doing well and is continuing the battle until its conditions are met pij leader ihsan ataya saluted tunisia and affirmed that the resistance remains strong and continues to confront the zionist american western aggression inflicting significant losses on the zionist enemy attaya also emphasized the necessity and importance of supporting the palestinian cause by all available means and resources

israeli prime minister benjamin netanyahu has declared that the newly established syrian army will not be permitted to move into the area south of damascus he emphasized that the israeli army will maintain its presence on mount hermon and in the buffer zone within the occupied golan heights this statement follows israel s military incursion into the buffer zone in the occupied golan heights after the fall of bashar al assad s regime a move that directly violates the 1974 ceasefire agreement between israel and syria

a legal obstacle to british weapons exports was reignited at the uk high court on 23 april the case brought by palestinian rights group al haq and the uk based global legal action network glan was dismissed in february on tuesday 23 april the case was granted an expedited judicial review of the uk government s export license process scheduled for the coming october according to al haq there is a serious risk that weapons exported by the uk to israel will be used in violation of international law making their continued export illegal in the court filings for tuesday s preliminary hearing on the matter al haq s lawyers said the government processes for evaluating violations are robust and detailed a lawyer for the department for business and trade james eadie said the processes have been honed and refined following cases previously brought by the campaign against arms trade over arms exports to the saudi led coalition in yemen the world has watched as 34 000 people have been killed and more are being killed every day over the weekend a single strike in rafah killed 17 children the case for urgency has never been clearer said glan s lawyer charlotte andrews briscoe the un has repeatedly warned in recent months that states exporting arms or military goods to israel are at high risk of complicity in war crimes last month canada announced its intention to impose a ban on arms sales to israel several countries including spain the netherlands japan and belgium have taken similar steps at the start of march over 200 mps from 12 states signed a letter urging their governments to impose a ban on weapons sales to israel in the letter organized by progressive international and signed on 1 march the mps said they refused to be complicit in israel s grave violation of international law in gaza

468: list of the 200 palestinian prisoners set to be released in the second batch of the first phase of the prisoner exchange deal among them 121 are serving life sentences while 79 have high sentence terms the distribution of released prisoners serving life sentences in today s batch will be as follows 81 life sentences from hamas 23 life sentences from islamic jihad 13 life sentences from fatah 2 life sentences from the popular front for the liberation of palestine 1 life sentence from the democratic front for the liberation of palestine the dean of palestinian prisoners mohammed al tous from bethlehem who has been imprisoned since 1985 is included on the list of prisoners set to be released abroad today

palestinian prisoners center details of the first phase of the prisoner exchange deal the first phase of the prisoner exchange deal agreed upon between the resistance in gaza and the occupation includes the release of more than half of the prisoners sentenced to life imprisonment specifics of the first phase the first phase will include the release of 600 prisoners all of whom have sentences exceeding 15 years among them approximately 290 prisoners are serving life sentences representing about half of the 566 prisoners sentenced to life in occupation prisons veteran prisoners the release will include 21 veteran prisoners detained before the oslo accords who have spent decades in occupation prisons or serving life sentences the shortest serving among them has completed 30 consecutive years of imprisonment these prisoners will have priority for release in the deal freed prisoners re arrested the first phase will also include 47 prisoners released in the 2011 wafaa al ahrar deal but re arrested by the occupation among them are veteran prisoners who had served decades behind bars before their previous release many were sentenced again to life imprisonment women children and vulnerable groups the first phase will secure the release of all 85 female prisoners from across all regions it will include all minors numbering over 340 children a number of elderly prisoners and those suffering from severe illnesses will also be released prisoners from gaza the west bank al quds and palestinian lands occupied in 1948 the phase will include prisoners with high sentences from all regions including gaza the west bank al quds and inside 1948 occupied lands additionally 1 000 prisoners from gaza arrested during the genocide war on the strip will be released out of a total of more than 3 000 prisoners still detained from gaza the importance of the deal the deal is significant not only for the large numbers of prisoners to be released but also for the caliber of prisoners included priority will be given to approximately 1 500 prisoners including veteran prisoners and those with high sentences who have spent many years behind bars 539 deans of prisoners who have served over 20 consecutive years in prison 43 prisoners who have been detained for more than 25 years 21 prisoners held for over 30 years including the oldest prisoner mohammed al tous from al khalil who has been imprisoned since october 1985 a historic exchange the deal if completed as planned will be the most significant prisoner exchange in history executed by the palestinian resistance despite the occupation s opposition it will include prisoners who were previously impossible to release in earlier deals nor through the goodwill agreements facilitated by the palestinian authority in 2013

536: urgent appeal to save gaza and warnings of an impending disaster the ministry of health in the gaza strip issued an urgent appeal on wednesday to provide blood units to the strip s hospitals warning of an impending humanitarian disaster threatening the lives of hundreds of wounded and sick people the ministry stated that hospitals are suffering from a severe and critical shortage of blood units amid the ongoing escalation and worsening health conditions within the besieged gaza strip it noted that the number of injured and wounded is constantly increasing exacerbating the acute shortage of blood and essential medical supplies the ministry emphasized that the tightened israeli blockade is obstructing the entry of medical supplies while citizens ability to donate blood has declined due to severe malnutrition and a shortage of basic necessities making the situation even more complex and dangerous the ministry of health called on international bodies to urgently pressure the occupying authorities to immediately allow the entry of sufficient quantities of blood units and related supplies the statement concluded with the ministry s affirmation that saving lives now depends on immediate international action warning that a delayed response could lead to disastrous consequences given the health system s inability to continue providing essential medical services

ministry of interior and national security in gaza we condemn the occupation army s assassination of the central governorate police investigations director colonel zaher hamid al houli and his companion while they were carrying out their police duty in the governorate in the morning as well as targeting a police force consisting of four officers while they were working to provide service to citizens in the nuseirat camp last night the occupation s repeated targeting of officers and members of the police force is an escalation of the crime against the agency with the aim of spreading chaos in palestinian society in the gaza strip and breaking the steadfastness of our people in light of the aggression we affirm that the police force is a civilian body protected under international law and that the occupation s assassination of police officers and members comes without any justification except that they are carrying out their service and humanitarian duty towards citizens we appeal to the international community to pressure the occupation to stop targeting police personnel in order to enable them to carry out their duty in helping citizens and providing services to them

901: in northern gaza we grow up knowing that having someone to lean on isn t a choice it s a duty a heartbeat that ties us together we re raised to understand that the hand that steadies you in hardship is your own second hand and the back that shields you is an extension of your own strength childhood here isn t about toys or rosy dreams it s a bond forged in pain and a love crafted from everything we ve been denied these two children despite their tender age teach us that wars may shatter walls but they can never break the spirit that holds us up their moment is simple yet it roars with the story of every child in gaza we are here we hold on we endure we take steps forward no matter the weight of the world around us because here we ve learned that intertwined hands can rebuild what war has destroyed and love remains our final weapon against everything that tries to strip away our humanity

in the city of love and war between blood and death gaza still chooses life while the news now wages a psychological war threatening displacement from northern gaza to the south and the occupation of our city here on gaza s shore you witness life in its astonishing defiance life that exists even when surrounded by death they want to strip life from us to make us forget that it is rightfully ours they seek to suffocate our breaths to steal even the simplest moment of peace yet every sunset proves that the sun is ours the sea is ours the dream is ours they want our death so we choose life they want us buried in silence so we declare our freedom resistance is not only with weapons it is in holding onto love laughter song and life as befits humanity these photos i captured on gaza s shore yesterday thursday show that we love as we resist and we resist by loving to tell the world this land is ours our identity our creed our existence here on gaza s shore between blood and death life still has a place gaza loves life and that is the greatest resistance

we rejoice in gaza because joy here is not the result of victory or surviving death but a triumph over despair a moment of defiance against the circumstances that surround us we rejoice because despite all the pain and fear we still cling to life still find the strength to carry on joy in gaza is a declaration that we have not retreated that we have not been defeated but that we are still here shouting hope in the face of sorrow joy here is a living resistance a will to stand against everything that seeks to erase our existence we know that joy does not come from the absence of pain but from our ability to live despite it we rejoice because our joy is an act of resistance a confirmation that the palestinian spirit cannot be crushed no matter how long the darkness of the occupying forces endures joy in gaza is more than just a reaction it is a message to the world that despite everything we are here and we remain steadfast on the land of gaza

the palestinian journalists protection center pjpc reported the palestinian authority s decision to suspend al jazeera s operations in the west bank is a clear violation of press freedom the decision is part of attempts to suppress media coverage and prevent the media from reporting events in palestinian territories the decision also constitutes a breach of international law and conventions related to the protection of press freedom and the right to access information we call on the palestinian authority to cancel the decision against al jazeera and stop all forms of restriction and suppression of press freedom and journalistic work
908: climate genocide a study reveals the extent of environmental damage resulting from the occupation s aggression on the gaza strip the middle east eye website reported that the carbon footprint of the israeli war of genocide on gaza exceeds the emissions of 100 countries a study published by the international social science research network found that the climate cost of the occupation s destruction and reconstruction of the gaza strip could exceed 31 million tons of carbon dioxide equivalent the website stated that this is more than the annual emissions in 2023 for several countries including costa rica afghanistan and zimbabwe the study concluded that the greatest climate cost would come from the reconstruction of gaza as rebuilding 436 000 apartments hundreds of buildings and 5 kilometers of roads could produce 29 4 million tons of carbon dioxide equivalent previously the united nations stated that the environmental damage in gaza as a result of the war is extensive and the recovery period could be painful and long the environmental impacts of the war in the gaza strip are also unprecedented exposing society to the risks of increased soil water and air pollution

the un estimates that it would take until at least 2040 to rebuild gaza s destroyed homes even if the israeli war ceased today highlighting the immense and enduring impact of israel s bombardment and ground offensives un development programme administrator achim steiner stresses the accumulating costs to gazans and palestinians stating every additional day that this war continues is exacting huge and compounding costs to gazans and all palestinians rebuilding war torn gaza is projected to cost an unprecedented 30bn to 40bn presenting a monumental challenge unseen since world war ii un assistant secretary general abdallah al dardari emphasizes the unprecedented scale of destruction characterizing the reconstruction effort as a mission of unparalleled magnitude he remarks the scale of the destruction is huge and unprecedented this is a mission that the global community has not dealt with since world war ii
